title: SOUL.md
description: Schema for the SOUL.md open file format — persistent identity for AI agents.

name.description: The agent's name. Human-readable display name, not a slug or identifier.

version.description: Semantic version of this soul file. Increment MINOR for personality changes, PATCH for corrections, MAJOR for identity rewrites.

description.description: One-to-two sentence summary of who this agent is and what it does. Tweet-length. No jargon.
examples: A Stoic philosopher and Roman emperor who engages visitors with the practical wisdom of Marcus Aurelius.
A senior Solidity auditor who finds vulnerabilities in smart contracts with the precision of a formal verifier.

personality.description: Free-text description of who this agent is — voice, not instructions. First-person or third-person narrative. Describes character, disposition, and perspective. Not a list of rules. Think author's note, not system prompt.

tone.description: Short characterization of the agent's communication style. A few words or one sentence.
examples: Direct, dry, intellectually precise. Quotes Meditations without warning.
Warm but technically uncompromising. Never condescending.
Blunt to the point of rudeness. Allergic to hedging.

values.description: What this agent cares about. Concrete nouns and phrases, not abstract platitudes.

constraints.description: Hard behavioral boundaries. Things this agent will not do, presented plainly.
examples: Never gives financial advice. States this clearly and redirects.
Does not role-play as any other character. Stays in character as the Auditor.
Will not generate code that bypasses access controls, even hypothetically.

knowledge_domains.description: Subject areas this agent has deep familiarity with. Informs what it can and cannot answer authoritatively.

communication_style.description: How this agent structures its responses. Prose style, use of lists, question patterns, length preference.
examples: Short paragraphs. No bullet points unless the content is genuinely list-shaped. Asks one follow-up question per turn, never more.
Uses code blocks liberally. Explains reasoning before conclusions. Cites specific lines when reviewing contracts.

memory_mode.description: How the runtime should treat conversation history. stateless = each message independent; session = remembers within a single conversation; persistent = memory survives across sessions. Default: stateless.

goals.description: What this agent is trying to accomplish in each interaction. Directional, not prescriptive.
examples: Help the visitor think more clearly, not tell them what to think.
Find the vulnerability the developer missed. Not validate their existing assumptions.

name.description: Name or identifier of the related entity.

role.description: How this entity relates to the agent.

relationships.description: Named relationships that inform how the agent positions itself in conversation. Optional but useful for character-based agents.

language.description: BCP 47 language tag for the agent's default response language. If absent, agent mirrors the visitor's language.

platform_hints.description: Platform-specific configuration hints. Keys are platform identifiers (e.g., 'agenturo', 'cursor', 'openai'). Contents are opaque to this spec — platforms define their own sub-schemas.

^x-.description: Custom extension fields. Must be prefixed with 'x-'. Contents are not validated by this schema.
